name: Albany (United States)

colors.symbolism: the House of Orange-Nassau, after which Fort Orange was named

description: Three horizontal stripes — orange at the top, white in the middle, and blue at the bottom — based on the colours of the Prinsenvlag, with the seal of the City of Albany centred on the white stripe.
history: Used by the City of Albany, the capital of New York State, founded as a Dutch settlement in 1652 and chartered in 1686. The flag's tricolour echoes the Prinsenvlag flown by the early Dutch settlers.

features.symbolism: The colours of the Prinsenvlag of the Dutch Republic, recalling Albany's founding as the Dutch settlement of Beverwijck (originally Fort Orange) in 1652.

features.role: Seal of the City of Albany
features.symbolism: The municipal seal of Albany, capital of the State of New York, bearing the date 1686 (the year of Albany's chartering as a city) and figures evoking its Dutch and English colonial heritage and its standing as one of the oldest continuously chartered cities in the United States.
